headline: WRLG HITS 215 g/t Au Over 5.35m At Red Lake — Stock Up 17% On Austin 904 Complex Bonanza
snippet: West Red Lake Gold (TSXV: WRLG) drilled 215.46 g/t Au over 5.35m at the Austin 904 Complex in Red Lake, Ontario. Multiple holes returned high-grade hits, pushing the stock up 16.9% with initial production from this area expected in H1 2027.

headline: $2.2 BILLION DEAL: G Mining Ventures Buys G2 Goldfields — Guyana District Targets 500,000 oz/yr
snippet: G Mining Ventures agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in an all-share deal valued at ~$2.2 billion, combining the Oko West and Oko-Ghanie deposits in Guyana. The district-scale complex targets 500,000+ oz Au/yr with $1B+ in projected synergies at an implied $618/oz valuation.

headline: GOLD SLIDES To $4,717 After Iran Talks Fail — JPMorgan Still Targeting $6,300 As Cycle High
snippet: Gold retreated to $4,717/oz after failed weekend Iran talks unwound ceasefire optimism, pulling back from a recent $4,850 high. JPMorgan holds a $6,300 cycle target while silver remains near $75.60 — up more than 30% over the past month — as the metals stay 46%+ above year-ago levels.

headline: Americas Gold & Silver FINDS 10 New Veins At Galena — Launches Record 64,000m Drill Campaign
snippet: Americas Gold & Silver discovered 10 new high-grade silver-copper-antimony and silver-lead veins at the Galena Complex, including 4,896 g/t Ag over 1.3m. The company is launching its largest-ever drill program — 64,000 metres across both Galena and Cosalá — in 2026.

headline: AbraSilver Drills 17m @ 432 g/t Ag At Diablillos JAC — DFS On Track As Resource Hits 186Moz Ag
snippet: AbraSilver's Phase V program at Diablillos JAC returned 17.0m @ 432 g/t Ag and 1.10 g/t Au, including a 6.0m sub-interval at 1,093 g/t Ag. The current M&I resource sits at 186Moz silver and 1.6Moz gold, with a definitive feasibility study targeted for Q2 2026.

headline: NexGen GREEN-LIGHTS Rook I Construction This Summer — Canada's Biggest Uranium Mine Nears Build
snippet: NexGen Energy received final federal approval for its Rook I uranium mine in Saskatchewan on March 5 and plans to begin construction this summer. The Arrow deposit could produce nearly 30 million lbs U3O8/year — roughly 20% of current global uranium supply and over 50% of western output.

headline: Core Lithium RESTARTS Finniss With $205M Secured — Ore Extraction May 2026, First Spodumene Q3
snippet: Core Lithium secured $205M in funding and approved the FID to restart its Finniss lithium project in Australia's Northern Territory. A $50M contract was awarded to NRW for surface mining at the Grants deposit, with ore extraction beginning May 2026 and first spodumene concentrate targeted for Q3 2026.

headline: CHINA IMPORTED 790+ Tonnes Of Silver In Early 2026 — Solar Factories Absorbing Record Output
snippet: China imported over 790 tonnes of physical silver in early 2026, driven largely by massive solar panel manufacturing. Silver used in photovoltaic paste has more than tripled in value over the past year, with the metal up 30%+ this month as industrial demand continues to outpace supply expectations.

headline: Silver Market Faces SIXTH Straight Supply Deficit — 148.9 Million Oz Shortfall Expected In 2026
snippet: The global silver market is on track for its sixth consecutive structural supply deficit in 2026. Last year's shortfall reached 148.9 million ounces as industrial demand from solar and electronics continued to outpace mine supply and recycling — with no structural fix visible near-term.

headline: COPPER Holds $5.87/Lb For Third Weekly Gain — LME Stockpiles At 8-Year High Cap The Rally
snippet: Copper futures held near $5.87/lb marking a third consecutive weekly advance, buoyed by US-Iran ceasefire optimism and Strait of Hormuz hopes. But LME warehouse stockpiles climbing to an 8-year high — alongside flare-ups in Lebanon — are keeping a ceiling on the rally.

headline: TRUMP LAUNCHES $12B Critical Minerals Stockpile — Rare Earth Stocks SURGE On 'Project Vault'
snippet: President Trump launched Project Vault, a $12B strategic critical minerals stockpile combining ~$2B in private capital with a $10B Ex-Im Bank loan targeting neodymium, dysprosium, and lithium. Rare earth stocks surged on the news — Critical Metals +35%, USA Rare Earth +11%, MP Materials +4%.

headline: GDXJ UP 203% In 12 Months As Gold Miners CRUSH The Metal — 'Super-Margin' Era Reshapes Sector
snippet: Gold mining equities have entered 'outperformance mode,' with GDX returning ~180% and GDXJ ~203% over the trailing 12 months as both ETFs smash all-time highs. AI-driven cost reductions are enabling 'super-margins' where miners capture outsized leverage to gold prices, prompting a structural sector re-rating.
